codeReview.summary: The feature removes the unsafe retry-time `killByPattern('node')` fallback and replaces it with Volt-scoped cleanup calls plus regression coverage that locks in the narrower behavior. The committed script still retries using only project-owned node processes, the debug sidecar executable, and known Volt dev ports, which matches the feature contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the stability-hardening procedure by reproducing the issue, narrowing the fix, and adding characterization coverage. The only deviation encountered was an initial test file placement under `scripts/`, but the worker corrected this within the session after discovering Vitest only includes `src/**/*.test.ts`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session c38a570d-8cd6-47a8-8f28-32828bf5d754 shows the failed targeted run against `scripts/cleanup-before-dev.test.mjs`, followed by relocating coverage to `src/lib/core/dev/cleanup-before-dev.test.ts`. `.factory/skills/stability-hardening-worker/SKILL.md` explicitly prefers targeted fixes with characterization tests.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, committed diff, and surrounding cleanup script/test. The implementation satisfies the requested scope reduction: no system-wide Node kill fallback remains, and the retry cleanup stays limited to Volt-owned processes and known dev ports.
